              f"@ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = ~{est_sec/60:.1f} min "
              f"(~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time")
        for ds, split, q, t in sets:
            n = len(q) * len(t)
            print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {n} regs "
                  f"(~{n*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min)")
    else:
        print("\n[cost] plain_mi: negligible per-pair cost; runs in seconds/pool.")

    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed caches make every volume load +
    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        ranker = RegMIRanker(grid=grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    else:
        ranker = MIRanker(grid=grid)

"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.

Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
isolation.

Rankers (--ranker):
  plain_mi  -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; default grid 64).
  reg_mi    -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; default grid 96).
               --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform. Registration is
               O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script prints an upfront
               cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.

Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
space-separated gallery, best->worst.

No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.

    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --datasets 2 \
        --out Alvaro/generated/mi_ds2.csv
    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --ranker reg_mi \
        --datasets 2 --splits val --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_ds2_val.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple

import pandas as pd

from .mi_ranker import MIRanker
from .reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID

ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
PER_REG_SEC = 0.49  # measured affine cost at 96^3 (single-threaded ANTs)


def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback (copied from Laurence).

    Our local disk holds uncompressed .nii while the CSVs name .nii.gz, so when the
    .gz path is absent we retry the bare .nii sibling.
    """
    p = Path(rel)
    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
        nii = p.with_suffix("")  # strip .gz -> try the .nii sibling
        if nii.exists():
            return nii
    return p


def _load_set(
    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
    """Load one (dataset, split) prediction set as ({qid: path}, {tid: path}).

    Returns None (with a printed warning) if either CSV is missing. Queries and
    targets whose image file is absent on disk are dropped with a warning, so a
    missing volume never aborts the whole run.
    """
    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
        return None

    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
            iid = str(row[id_col])
            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
            if p.exists():
                present[iid] = p
            else:
                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
        return present

    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
    if not queries or not targets:
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
        return None
    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
    return queries, targets


def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
                  label: str) -> None:
    """Eyeball that the ranker discriminates: top-3 + worst score, first 2 queries.

    Works for any ranker exposing score_targets (MIRanker / RegMIRanker). For
    reg_mi the printed NMI is POST-registration, which is what we want to eyeball
    on real data.
    """
    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)  # [(tid, nmi)] desc
        top3 = scored[:3]
        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
        print(f"  query {qid}:")
        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
        if spread < 0.02:  # NMI lives in ~[1,2]; a real signal spreads far more
            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=64,
                    help="resample cube edge passed to the MI ranker (default 64)")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = [int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip()]
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  downsample={args.downsample}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (so the sanity block can run
    #    on dataset1/val before we commit to the full, slower ranking pass).
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in SPLITS:
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries we will actually rank

    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed cache makes every volume load +
    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
    ranker = MIRanker(grid=args.downsample)

    # 2) Sanity check BEFORE writing: prefer dataset1/val, else the first set.
    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
                   if d == 1 and s == "val"), None)
    if sanity is None:
        d, s, q, t = sets[0]
        sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}")
    _sanity_block(ranker, sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 3) Rank every query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only.
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    done = 0
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        for qid, qpath in queries.items():
            ranking = ranker.rank(qid, str(qpath), str_targets)  # full gallery, best->worst
            # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
            done += 1
            if done % 10 == 0:
                print(f"  ranked {done}/{n_expected} queries "
                      f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # 4) Write + verify. Row count must equal the queries we set out to rank.
    assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    out_path = Path(args.out)
    if not out_path.is_absolute():
        # Keep relative outputs anchored at the repo root, not the CWD.
        out_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / out_path
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}  "

file.content:
n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries we will actually rank

    # 1b) Upfront cost estimate. reg_mi does N_queries * N_targets registrations per
    #     pool; print the projection BEFORE any work so a mis-scoped run can be
    #     aborted. plain_mi's per-pair cost is negligible (vectorized histograms).
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        total_regs = sum(len(q) * len(t) for _, _, q, t in sets)
        est_sec = total_regs * PER_REG_SEC
        print(f"\n[cost] reg_mi: {total_regs} registrations "
              f"@ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = ~{est_sec/60:.1f} min "
              f"(~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time")
        for ds, split, q, t in sets:
            n = len(q) * len(t)
            print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {n} regs "
                  f"(~{n*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min)")
    else:
        print("\n[cost] plain_mi: negligible per-pair cost; runs in seconds/pool.")

    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed caches make every volume load +
    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        ranker = RegMIRanker(grid=grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    else:
        ranker = MIRanker(grid=grid)

    # 2) Sanity check BEFORE writing: prefer dataset1/val, else the first set.
    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
                   if d == 1 and s == "val"), None)
    if sanity is None:
        d, s, q, t = sets[0]
        sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}")
    _sanity_block(ranker, sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 3) Rank every query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only.
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    done = 0
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        for qid, qpath in queries.items():
            ranking = ranker.rank(qid, str(qpath), str_targets)  # full gallery, best->worst
            # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
            done += 1

content.content:
syntax OK
usage: make_submission.py [-h] --data-root DATA_ROOT [--out OUT]
                          [--datasets DATASETS] [--splits SPLITS]
                          [--ranker {plain_mi,reg_mi}] [--transform TRANSFORM]
                          [--downsample DOWNSAMPLE]

Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.
Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers
so we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted
queries as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or
single-split) file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's
real MRR in isolation. Rankers (--ranker): plain_mi -- mi_ranker.MIRanker
(fast; default grid 64). reg_mi -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-
then-MI; default grid 96). --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform.
Registration is O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script prints an
upfront cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work. Pool
isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
space-separated gallery, best->worst. No network, no Kaggle API -- this only
writes a CSV locally. python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root .
--datasets 2 --out Alvaro/generated/mi_ds2.csv python -m
Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --ranker reg_mi --datasets 2 --splits
val --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_ds2_val.csv

content.text:
The cost estimate and ds2-val sanity block are in; the 13-min ranking pass is now running (I'll confirm the file when it completes).

## Upfront cost estimate (printed before any work, as requested)
```
[cost] reg_mi: 1600 registrations @ ~0.49s = ~13.1 min (~0.22 h) projected wall-time
       dataset2/val: 40x40 = 1600 regs (~13.1 min)
```

## ds2-val sanity block — post-registration NMI on REAL data
```
query q_35e5b99d921d:
  #1: g_da4d95302db1  NMI=1.1697
  #2: g_a66ca8859c39  NMI=1.1549
  #3: g_6afaaa245b15  NMI=1.1545
  worst: g_d7bd864b93b4  NMI=1.0856   (top-tail spread=0.0841)
query q_3cedbdbcd6b6:
  #1: g_8f00f3e70789  NMI=1.1506
  #2: g_4d6776380fd9  NMI=1.1477
  #3: g_a66ca8859c39  NMI=1.1430
  worst: g_0ac82d7f9d83  NMI=1.0929   (top-tail spread=0.0578)
```

**Read:** registration is discriminating on real ds2 — top-vs-tail spread ~0.06–0.08, the same healthy magnitude as the proxy (and well above the 0.02 degenerate floor). Caveat: real ds2 has no labels, so this only confirms *separation*, not that #1 is the true target — that's exactly what the Kaggle submission will tell us.

## What changed (all inside `Alvaro/`)
- `make_submission.py`: added `--ranker {plain_mi,reg_mi}`, `--transform`, `--splits`, ranker-aware `--downsample` default (64 plain / 96 reg), and the upfront O(N²) cost estimate. Pool isolation, resolve fallback, full-length rankings, row-count assertion, sanity block, progress+timing all unchanged.
- `reg_mi_ranker.py`: added `RegMIRanker.score_targets()` (so the sanity block shows post-registration NMI); `rank()` now delegates to it — backward-compatible.

I did **not** launch ds2 test or the full submission. The run will write **`Alvaro/generated/regmi_ds2_val.csv`** (expect 40 rows) when it finishes in ~13 min — I'll confirm the path and row count on completion. Then per your branch plan: real ds2-val ~0.85+ → scale affine to all test rows; ~0.5 → switch to SyN.

- **Structure validated:** 40/40 queries present and matching the ds2-val query set; every ranking is full-length (40 targets), no duplicates, full gallery coverage, zero cross-pool leakage. Valid Kaggle format.
- **Sanity (from the run):** post-registration NMI separates top from tail on real ds2 (spread ~0.06–0.08), same healthy magnitude as the proxy.

This is the file to **submit in isolation** to read real ds2 MRR (Kaggle's omitted=0, ×3 rule means the reported leaderboard number ÷ ... actually the displayed score = ds2_MRR/3 since ds1/ds3 are omitted=0; multiply the shown score by 3 to recover ds2_MRR).

## Branch on the real number (per your plan)
- **ds2-val ≈ 0.85+** → affine generalizes to real data; next step is scaling affine to all test rows. Cost preview for the full affine submission (the script will print it upfront): ds1 test 100×100 + ds2 test 100×100 + ds3 test 77×77 ≈ 25,929 regs ≈ **~3.5 h** single-threaded (parallelizable across cores), plus val sets.
- **ds2-val ≈ 0.5** → affine isn't enough on real data; switch to `--transform SyN` (the flag is wired) and re-check ds2-val before committing the slower SyN to test.

Nothing else launched. Submit `regmi_ds2_val.csv`, and tell me the score — I'll scope the next run accordingly.

message.content:
Read Alvaro/src/make_submission.py and Alvaro/src/reg_mi_ranker.py. Work ONLY inside Alvaro/.

CONTEXT: real ds2-val with affine reg-then-MI scored 0.884 (0.2947 x3). Affine
generalizes — we are scaling it to the full real submission. NOT switching to SyN.

GOAL: one combined Kaggle submission, reg-MI affine grid 96, covering ALL rows:
ds1/ds2/ds3 x {val,test}. But ds1 and ds3 do NOT need registration (plain MI already
scores ~0.98 on both, and registering aligned images cost a tiny -0.019). So:
  - ds1 (val+test): plain MI  (fast, 64^3)
  - ds3 (val+test): plain MI  (fast, 64^3)
  - ds2 (val+test): reg-MI affine (96^3)  <-- the only registered pool
This avoids ~16k pointless registrations (ds1 100x100 + ds3 77x77) and cuts runtime
to just ds2: 40x40 + 100x100 = 11,600 regs (~95 min single-threaded).

Implement a per-dataset ranker choice in make_submission.py (a --ranker-map or
similar) so one run emits the full combined CSV with plain MI for ds1/ds3 and reg-MI
for ds2. Keep all existing guarantees: pool isolation, .nii.gz/.nii resolve,
full-length rankings, row-count assertion (expect 377 total), sanity blocks, upfront
cost estimate, progress + timing.

PARALLELISM: ds2 is the bottleneck and is embarrassingly parallel over queries. If
running locally would exceed ~90 min, structure the ds2 pass so it can be sharded
(process a --query-shard i/N slice and write a partial), then a merge step combines
shards + the plain-MI ds1/ds3 rows into the final 377-row CSV. If you can run it on
Laurence's Modal workspace (modal is installed; see laurence/*.py for the volume
ehl-2026-vol-2 mount pattern), prefer that — fan ds2 queries across containers. Your
call on local-shard vs Modal; tell me which and the projected wall-time BEFORE
launching.

Print the upfront cost estimate and STOP for my confirmation before the long run.
Output: Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv (377 rows).

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.
2	
3	Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
4	({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
5	we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
6	as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
7	file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
8	isolation.
9	
10	Rankers (--ranker):
11	  plain_mi  -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; default grid 64).
12	  reg_mi    -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; default grid 96).
13	               --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform. Registration is
14	               O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script prints an upfront
15	               cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.
16	
17	Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
18	dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
19	space-separated gallery, best->worst.
20	
21	No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.
22	
23	    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --datasets 2 \
24	        --out Alvaro/generated/mi_ds2.csv
25	    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --ranker reg_mi \
26	        --datasets 2 --splits val --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_ds2_val.csv
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import argparse
32	import time
33	from pathlib import Path
34	from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
35	
36	import pandas as pd
37	
38	from .mi_ranker import MIRanker
39	from .reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID
40	
41	ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
42	SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
43	PER_REG_SEC = 0.49  # measured affine cost at 96^3 (single-threaded ANTs)
44	
45	
46	def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
47	    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback (copied from Laurence).
48	
49	    Our local disk holds uncompressed .nii while the CSVs name .nii.gz, so when the
50	    .gz path is absent we retry the bare .nii sibling.
51	    """
52	    p = Path(rel)
53	    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
54	    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
55	        nii = p.with_suffix("")  # strip .gz -> try the .nii sibling
56	        if nii.exists():
57	            return nii
58	    return p
59	
60	
61	def _load_set(
62	    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
63	) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
64	    """Load one (dataset, split) prediction set as ({qid: path}, {tid: path}).
65	
66	    Returns None (with a printed warning) if either CSV is missing. Queries and
67	    targets whose image file is absent on disk are dropped with a warning, so a
68	    missing volume never aborts the whole run.
69	    """
70	    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
71	    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
72	    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
73	    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
74	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
75	        return None
76	
77	    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
78	        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
79	        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
80	            iid = str(row[id_col])
81	            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
82	            if p.exists():
83	                present[iid] = p
84	            else:
85	                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
86	        return present
87	
88	    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
89	    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
90	    if not queries or not targets:
91	        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
92	        return None
93	    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
94	    return queries, targets
95	
96	
97	def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
98	                  label: str) -> None:
99	    """Eyeball that the ranker discriminates: top-3 + worst score, first 2 queries.
100	
101	    Works for any ranker exposing score_targets (MIRanker / RegMIRanker). For
102	    reg_mi the printed NMI is POST-registration, which is what we want to eyeball
103	    on real data.
104	    """
105	    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
106	    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
107	    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
108	        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)  # [(tid, nmi)] desc
109	        top3 = scored[:3]
110	        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
111	        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
112	        print(f"  query {qid}:")
113	        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
114	            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
115	        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
116	        if spread < 0.02:  # NMI lives in ~[1,2]; a real signal spreads far more
117	            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")
118	
119	
120	def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
121	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
122	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
123	                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
124	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
125	    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
126	                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
127	    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
128	                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
129	    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=["plain_mi", "reg_mi"], default="plain_mi",
130	                    help="plain_mi (fast) or reg_mi (ANTs register-then-MI)")
131	    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
132	                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
133	    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
134	                    help="resample cube edge; default 64 for plain_mi, 96 for reg_mi")
135	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
136	
137	    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
138	    ds_ids = [int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip()]
139	    splits = [s.strip() for s in args.splits.split(",") if s.strip()]
140	    # Ranker-aware default grid: registration needs more detail than plain MI.
141	    grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else (
142	        REG_GRID if args.ranker == "reg_mi" else 64)
143	    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}  "
144	          f"ranker={args.ranker}  transform={args.transform}  grid={grid}")
145	
146	    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (so the sanity block can run
147	    #    before we commit to the full, slower ranking pass).
148	    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
149	    for ds in ds_ids:
150	        for split in splits:
151	            if split not in ALL_SPLITS:
152	                print(f"  WARN unknown split '{split}'; skipping")
153	                continue
154	            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
155	            if loaded is not None:
156	                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
157	    if not sets:
158	        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")
159	
160	    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries we will actually rank
161	
162	    # 1b) Upfront cost estimate. reg_mi does N_queries * N_targets registrations per
163	    #     pool; print the projection BEFORE any work so a mis-scoped run can be
164	    #     aborted. plain_mi's per-pair cost is negligible (vectorized histograms).
165	    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
166	        total_regs = sum(len(q) * len(t) for _, _, q, t in sets)
167	        est_sec = total_regs * PER_REG_SEC
168	        print(f"\n[cost] reg_mi: {total_regs} registrations "
169	              f"@ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = ~{est_sec/60:.1f} min "
170	              f"(~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time")
171	        for ds, split, q, t in sets:
172	            n = len(q) * len(t)
173	            print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {n} regs "
174	                  f"(~{n*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min)")
175	    else:
176	        print("\n[cost] plain_mi: negligible per-pair cost; runs in seconds/pool.")
177	
178	    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed caches make every volume load +
179	    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
180	    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
181	        ranker = RegMIRanker(grid=grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
182	    else:
183	        ranker = MIRanker(grid=grid)
184	
185	    # 2) Sanity check BEFORE writing: prefer dataset1/val, else the first set.
186	    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
187	                   if d == 1 and s == "val"), None)
188	    if sanity is None:
189	        d, s, q, t = sets[0]
190	        sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}")
191	    _sanity_block(ranker, sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])
192	
193	    # 3) Rank every query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only.
194	    print("\n=== ranking ===")
195	    t0 = time.time()
196	    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
197	    done = 0
198	    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
199	        str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
200	        for qid, qpath in queries.items():
201	            ranking = ranker.rank(qid, str(qpath), str_targets)  # full gallery, best->worst
202	            # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
203	            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
204	            done += 1
205	            if done % 10 == 0:
206	                print(f"  ranked {done}/{n_expected} queries "
207	                      f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)
208	
209	    # 4) Write + verify. Row count must equal the queries we set out to rank.
210	    assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
211	    out_path = Path(args.out)
212	    if not out_path.is_absolute():
213	        # Keep relative outputs anchored at the repo root, not the CWD.
214	        out_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / out_path
215	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
216	    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(out_path, index=False)
217	    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}  "
218	          f"(total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
219	
220	
221	if __name__ == "__main__":
222	    main()
223

"""Build a Kaggle-format submission by running the MI ranker over the REAL data.

Mirrors the six prediction sets of laurence/dinov2_baseline_modal.py
({dataset1,2,3} x {val,test}), but ranks with one of our training-free rankers so
we can submit and read the real per-dataset numbers. Kaggle scores omitted queries
as 0 and averages ds1/ds2/ds3 x3, so writing a single-dataset (or single-split)
file (e.g. --datasets 2 --splits val) lets us read that pool's real MRR in
isolation.

Rankers (--ranker):
  plain_mi  -- mi_ranker.MIRanker (fast; default grid 64).
  reg_mi    -- reg_mi_ranker.RegMIRanker (ANTs register-then-MI; default grid 96).
               --transform {Affine,SyN} picks the ANTs transform. Registration is
               O(N_queries * N_targets) per pool, so the script prints an upfront
               cost estimate and projected wall-time BEFORE doing any work.

Pool isolation is the key invariant: every query is ranked ONLY against its own
dataset's own split gallery; we never mix pools. Each ranking is the full
space-separated gallery, best->worst.

No network, no Kaggle API -- this only writes a CSV locally.

    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --datasets 2 \
        --out Alvaro/generated/mi_ds2.csv
    python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --ranker reg_mi \
        --datasets 2 --splits val --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_ds2_val.csv
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import time
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple

import pandas as pd

from .mi_ranker import MIRanker
from .reg_mi_ranker import RegMIRanker, REG_GRID

ALL_SPLITS = ("val", "test")
SUBMISSION_COLS = ["query_id", "target_id_ranking"]
PER_REG_SEC = 0.49  # measured affine cost at 96^3 (single-threaded ANTs)


def resolve(data_root: Path, rel: str) -> Path:
    """Absolute image path with the .nii.gz -> .nii fallback (copied from Laurence).

    Our local disk holds uncompressed .nii while the CSVs name .nii.gz, so when the
    .gz path is absent we retry the bare .nii sibling.
    """
    p = Path(rel)
    p = p if p.is_absolute() else data_root / p
    if not p.exists() and p.suffix == ".gz":
        nii = p.with_suffix("")  # strip .gz -> try the .nii sibling
        if nii.exists():
            return nii
    return p


def _load_set(
    data_root: Path, ds: int, split: str
) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]] | None:
    """Load one (dataset, split) prediction set as ({qid: path}, {tid: path}).

    Returns None (with a printed warning) if either CSV is missing. Queries and
    targets whose image file is absent on disk are dropped with a warning, so a
    missing volume never aborts the whole run.
    """
    ds_dir = data_root / f"dataset{ds}"
    qcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_queries.csv"
    gcsv = ds_dir / f"{split}_gallery.csv"
    if not qcsv.exists() or not gcsv.exists():
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing CSV ({qcsv.name}/{gcsv.name}); skipping set")
        return None

    def collect(csv_path: Path, id_col: str, img_col: str) -> Dict[str, Path]:
        present: Dict[str, Path] = {}
        for _, row in pd.read_csv(csv_path).iterrows():
            iid = str(row[id_col])
            p = resolve(data_root, str(row[img_col]))
            if p.exists():
                present[iid] = p
            else:
                print(f"    WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: missing image for {iid}: {p}")
        return present

    queries = collect(qcsv, "query_id", "query_image")
    targets = collect(gcsv, "target_id", "target_image")
    if not queries or not targets:
        print(f"  WARN dataset{ds}/{split}: no usable queries/targets on disk; skipping set")
        return None
    print(f"  dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(queries)} queries, {len(targets)} targets")
    return queries, targets


def _sanity_block(ranker, queries: Dict[str, Path], targets: Dict[str, Path],
                  label: str) -> None:
    """Eyeball that the ranker discriminates: top-3 + worst score, first 2 queries.

    Works for any ranker exposing score_targets (MIRanker / RegMIRanker). For
    reg_mi the printed NMI is POST-registration, which is what we want to eyeball
    on real data.
    """
    print(f"\n=== SANITY ({label}) — does the score separate top from tail? ===")
    str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
    for qid in list(queries)[:2]:
        scored = ranker.score_targets(str(queries[qid]), str_targets)  # [(tid, nmi)] desc
        top3 = scored[:3]
        worst_tid, worst_score = scored[-1]
        spread = top3[0][1] - worst_score
        print(f"  query {qid}:")
        for rank, (tid, sc) in enumerate(top3, 1):
            print(f"    #{rank}: {tid}  NMI={sc:.4f}")
        print(f"    worst: {worst_tid}  NMI={worst_score:.4f}   (top-tail spread={spread:.4f})")
        if spread < 0.02:  # NMI lives in ~[1,2]; a real signal spreads far more
            print("    !! DEGENERATE: top barely above tail — MI is not discriminating here.")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=["plain_mi", "reg_mi"], default="plain_mi",
                    help="plain_mi (fast) or reg_mi (ANTs register-then-MI)")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="resample cube edge; default 64 for plain_mi, 96 for reg_mi")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = [int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip()]
    splits = [s.strip() for s in args.splits.split(",") if s.strip()]
    # Ranker-aware default grid: registration needs more detail than plain MI.
    grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else (
        REG_GRID if args.ranker == "reg_mi" else 64)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}  "
          f"ranker={args.ranker}  transform={args.transform}  grid={grid}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (so the sanity block can run
    #    before we commit to the full, slower ranking pass).
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in splits:
            if split not in ALL_SPLITS:
                print(f"  WARN unknown split '{split}'; skipping")
                continue
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries we will actually rank

    # 1b) Upfront cost estimate. reg_mi does N_queries * N_targets registrations per
    #     pool; print the projection BEFORE any work so a mis-scoped run can be
    #     aborted. plain_mi's per-pair cost is negligible (vectorized histograms).
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        total_regs = sum(len(q) * len(t) for _, _, q, t in sets)
        est_sec = total_regs * PER_REG_SEC
        print(f"\n[cost] reg_mi: {total_regs} registrations "
              f"@ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = ~{est_sec/60:.1f} min "
              f"(~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time")
        for ds, split, q, t in sets:
            n = len(q) * len(t)
            print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {n} regs "
                  f"(~{n*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min)")
    else:
        print("\n[cost] plain_mi: negligible per-pair cost; runs in seconds/pool.")

    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed caches make every volume load +
    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        ranker = RegMIRanker(grid=grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    else:
        ranker = MIRanker(grid=grid)

    # 2) Sanity check BEFORE writing: prefer dataset1/val, else the first set.
    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
                   if d == 1 and s == "val"), None)
    if sanity is None:
        d, s, q, t = sets[0]
        sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}")
    _sanity_block(ranker, sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 3) Rank every query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only.
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    done = 0
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        for qid, qpath in queries.items():
            ranking = ranker.rank(qid, str(qpath), str_targets)  # full gallery, best->worst
            # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
            done += 1
            if done % 10 == 0:
                print(f"  ranked {done}/{n_expected} queries "
                      f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # 4) Write + verify. Row count must equal the queries we set out to rank.
    assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    out_path = Path(args.out)
    if not out_path.is_absolute():
        # Keep relative outputs anchored at the repo root, not the CWD.
        out_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / out_path
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}  "
          f"(total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

aiTitle: Set up local MRI retrieval evaluation framework

content.text:
I have `reg_mi_ranker.py` in context. Before writing, two findings that shape the design decision:

**Local parallelism reality:** the ds2-val run logged `672% cpu` — ANTs/ITK already multithreads each registration across ~6.7 cores. So **local process-sharding gives no speedup** (parallel shards just oversubscribe the same cores). The local floor for ds2 (11,600 regs × 0.49s wall) is **~95 min** regardless of sharding. Sharding only buys a real speedup on **Modal** (more machines) or buys **resumability** locally.

**Modal vs local — my call: run LOCAL single-process (~95 min).** Reasons: the affine path is already validated end-to-end locally (ds2-val → 0.884); Modal would need a fresh antspyx image build, unverified confirmation that `ehl-2026-vol-2` holds the real data, and remote auth/debug — risk I can't bound, against a theoretical ~15-min win. 95 min is only marginally over 90.

I'll still **implement** the per-dataset ranker map **and** `--query-shard i/N` + a merge mode — the shard mechanism gives resumability for the 95-min run and is the hook for Modal later if you decide the speedup is worth the risk. Then I print the cost estimate and **stop**.

Let me update the docstring and rewrite `main()` with helpers.

input.new_string:
def _abs_out(out: str) -> Path:
    """Anchor a relative --out at the repo root, not the CWD."""
    p = Path(out)
    return p if p.is_absolute() else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / p


def _shard_path(out_path: Path, i: int, n: int) -> Path:
    """Partial-CSV path for shard i of n (1-indexed), beside the final --out."""
    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")


def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
    """Per-dataset ranker name. --ranker-map 'ds:name,...' overrides --ranker default."""
    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
    if spec:
        for item in spec.split(","):
            if not item.strip():
                continue
            ds_str, name = item.split(":")
            name = name.strip()
            if name not in ("plain_mi", "reg_mi"):
                raise SystemExit(f"--ranker-map: unknown ranker '{name}'")
            mapping[int(ds_str)] = name
    return mapping


def _merge_shards(out_path: Path, n: int, n_expected: int, expected_ids: set) -> None:
    """Concatenate the n shard partials into the final --out, checking coverage."""
    frames = []
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        sp = _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
        if not sp.exists():
            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")
        frames.append(pd.read_csv(sp))
    merged = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    ids = set(merged["query_id"].astype(str))
    dups = len(merged) - merged["query_id"].nunique()
    if dups:
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: {dups} duplicate query rows across shards")
    if ids != expected_ids:
        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")
    assert len(merged) == n_expected, f"merged {len(merged)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    merged[SUBMISSION_COLS].to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=["plain_mi", "reg_mi"], default="plain_mi",
                    help="ranker for ALL datasets unless overridden by --ranker-map")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker-map", default=None,
                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")
    ap.add_argument("--query-shard", default="1/1",
                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")
    ap.add_argument("--merge-shards", type=int, default=0,
                    help="merge N shard partials into --out and exit")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]  # canonical val,test order
    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"
          f"ranker_map={ranker_of_ds}  transform={args.transform}  "
          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (deterministic order: dataset
    #    ascending, then val before test) so shard slicing is reproducible.
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in splits:
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries across all sets
    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}

    # Merge mode: combine existing shard partials and exit (no ranking).
    if args.merge_shards:
        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
        return

    # 2) Flatten to a deterministic global query list, tagging each with its pool's
    #    ranker. str_targets is built once per set and shared by reference.
    flat: List[Tuple[int, str, str, str, Dict[str, str], str]] = []
    set_str_targets = []
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        set_str_targets.append(st)
        for qid in sorted(queries):  # sort -> reproducible shard membership
            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))

    # 3) Round-robin shard selection (so the ~140 expensive ds2 queries spread
    #    evenly across shards rather than piling into one contiguous chunk).
    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
    if not (1 <= i <= n):
        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

    # 4) Upfront cost estimate over THIS shard's queries (reg pairs dominate; plain
    #    pairs are negligible). Print BEFORE any heavy work so it can be aborted.
    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
    est_sec = reg_pairs * PER_REG_SEC
    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
          f"{sum(rn=='plain_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} plain)")
    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} registrations @ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = "
          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
          f"(+ plain MI negligible)")
    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
        tag = (f"~{npairs*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
               else "negligible (plain)")
        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")

    # 5) Build only the ranker instances this shard actually needs (RegMIRanker
    #    imports ANTs, so skip it if no reg pool is in play). Caches are per-instance
    #    and reused across the shard's queries.
    need = {rn for *_, rn in shard}
    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}
    if "plain_mi" in need:
        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
    if "reg_mi" in need:
        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)

    # 6) Sanity block (only shard 1, to avoid every container repeating it): prefer
    #    a reg pool (that's the risky ranker), else dataset1/val, else first set.
    if i == 1:
        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
        if sanity is None:
            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 7) Rank each query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only (pool isolation).
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)  # full gallery best->worst
        # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # 8) Write + verify. Single run (N=1) writes --out and must total n_expected;
    #    a shard writes a partial and must total this shard's query count.
    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(dest, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {dest}  (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
    if n == 1:
        assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"total {len(rows)} != expected {n_expected}"
    else:
        print(f"Shard {i}/{n} done. Merge with: --merge-shards {n} --out {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=["plain_mi", "reg_mi"], default="plain_mi",
                    help="plain_mi (fast) or reg_mi (ANTs register-then-MI)")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="resample cube edge; default 64 for plain_mi, 96 for reg_mi")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = [int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip()]
    splits = [s.strip() for s in args.splits.split(",") if s.strip()]
    # Ranker-aware default grid: registration needs more detail than plain MI.
    grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else (
        REG_GRID if args.ranker == "reg_mi" else 64)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}  "
          f"ranker={args.ranker}  transform={args.transform}  grid={grid}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (so the sanity block can run
    #    before we commit to the full, slower ranking pass).
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in splits:
            if split not in ALL_SPLITS:
                print(f"  WARN unknown split '{split}'; skipping")
                continue
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries we will actually rank

    # 1b) Upfront cost estimate. reg_mi does N_queries * N_targets registrations per
    #     pool; print the projection BEFORE any work so a mis-scoped run can be
    #     aborted. plain_mi's per-pair cost is negligible (vectorized histograms).
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        total_regs = sum(len(q) * len(t) for _, _, q, t in sets)
        est_sec = total_regs * PER_REG_SEC
        print(f"\n[cost] reg_mi: {total_regs} registrations "
              f"@ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = ~{est_sec/60:.1f} min "
              f"(~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time")
        for ds, split, q, t in sets:
            n = len(q) * len(t)
            print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split}: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {n} regs "
                  f"(~{n*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min)")
    else:
        print("\n[cost] plain_mi: negligible per-pair cost; runs in seconds/pool.")

    # One ranker for the whole run: its path-keyed caches make every volume load +
    # downsample happen exactly once, even when a target recurs across all queries.
    if args.ranker == "reg_mi":
        ranker = RegMIRanker(grid=grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)
    else:
        ranker = MIRanker(grid=grid)

    # 2) Sanity check BEFORE writing: prefer dataset1/val, else the first set.
    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
                   if d == 1 and s == "val"), None)
    if sanity is None:
        d, s, q, t = sets[0]
        sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}")
    _sanity_block(ranker, sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 3) Rank every query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only.
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    done = 0
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        str_targets = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        for qid, qpath in queries.items():
            ranking = ranker.rank(qid, str(qpath), str_targets)  # full gallery, best->worst
            # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
            rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
            done += 1
            if done % 10 == 0:
                print(f"  ranked {done}/{n_expected} queries "
                      f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # 4) Write + verify. Row count must equal the queries we set out to rank.
    assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    out_path = Path(args.out)
    if not out_path.is_absolute():
        # Keep relative outputs anchored at the repo root, not the CWD.
        out_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / out_path
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {out_path}  "
          f"(total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def _abs_out(out: str) -> Path:
    """Anchor a relative --out at the repo root, not the CWD."""
    p = Path(out)
    return p if p.is_absolute() else Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / p


def _shard_path(out_path: Path, i: int, n: int) -> Path:
    """Partial-CSV path for shard i of n (1-indexed), beside the final --out."""
    return out_path.with_name(f"{out_path.stem}.shard{i}-of-{n}{out_path.suffix}")


def _parse_ranker_map(spec: str | None, default_name: str, ds_ids: List[int]) -> Dict[int, str]:
    """Per-dataset ranker name. --ranker-map 'ds:name,...' overrides --ranker default."""
    mapping = {ds: default_name for ds in ds_ids}
    if spec:
        for item in spec.split(","):
            if not item.strip():
                continue
            ds_str, name = item.split(":")
            name = name.strip()
            if name not in ("plain_mi", "reg_mi"):
                raise SystemExit(f"--ranker-map: unknown ranker '{name}'")
            mapping[int(ds_str)] = name
    return mapping


def _merge_shards(out_path: Path, n: int, n_expected: int, expected_ids: set) -> None:
    """Concatenate the n shard partials into the final --out, checking coverage."""
    frames = []
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        sp = _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
        if not sp.exists():
            raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: missing shard partial {sp}")
        frames.append(pd.read_csv(sp))
    merged = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    ids = set(merged["query_id"].astype(str))
    dups = len(merged) - merged["query_id"].nunique()
    if dups:
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: {dups} duplicate query rows across shards")
    if ids != expected_ids:
        missing, extra = expected_ids - ids, ids - expected_ids
        raise SystemExit(f"--merge-shards: coverage mismatch "
                         f"(missing {len(missing)}, extra {len(extra)})")
    assert len(merged) == n_expected, f"merged {len(merged)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
    merged[SUBMISSION_COLS].to_csv(out_path, index=False)
    print(f"Merged {n} shards -> {len(merged)} rows at {out_path}")


def main(argv: List[str] | None = None) -> None:
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True,
                    help="root that the CSV image paths are relative to")
    ap.add_argument("--out", default="Alvaro/generated/mi_submission.csv")
    ap.add_argument("--datasets", default="1,2,3",
                    help="comma-separated dataset ids to include (e.g. '2')")
    ap.add_argument("--splits", default="val,test",
                    help="comma-separated splits to include (default 'val,test')")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker", choices=["plain_mi", "reg_mi"], default="plain_mi",
                    help="ranker for ALL datasets unless overridden by --ranker-map")
    ap.add_argument("--ranker-map", default=None,
                    help="per-dataset override, e.g. '1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi'")
    ap.add_argument("--transform", default="Affine",
                    help="ANTs type_of_transform for reg_mi (Affine, SyN, ...)")
    ap.add_argument("--downsample", type=int, default=None,
                    help="reg_mi grid override (default 96); plain_mi is always 64")
    ap.add_argument("--query-shard", default="1/1",
                    help="process the i-th of N round-robin query slices (e.g. 2/4)")
    ap.add_argument("--merge-shards", type=int, default=0,
                    help="merge N shard partials into --out and exit")
    args = ap.parse_args(argv)

    data_root = Path(args.data_root).resolve()
    ds_ids = sorted(int(x) for x in args.datasets.split(",") if x.strip())
    splits = [s for s in ALL_SPLITS if s in
              {x.strip() for x in args.splits.split(",")}]  # canonical val,test order
    ranker_of_ds = _parse_ranker_map(args.ranker_map, args.ranker, ds_ids)
    reg_grid = args.downsample if args.downsample is not None else REG_GRID
    out_path = _abs_out(args.out)
    print(f"data-root={data_root}  datasets={ds_ids}  splits={splits}\n"
          f"ranker_map={ranker_of_ds}  transform={args.transform}  "
          f"plain_grid=64 reg_grid={reg_grid}")

    # 1) Load every requested prediction set up front (deterministic order: dataset
    #    ascending, then val before test) so shard slicing is reproducible.
    sets: List[Tuple[int, str, Dict[str, Path], Dict[str, Path]]] = []
    for ds in ds_ids:
        for split in splits:
            loaded = _load_set(data_root, ds, split)
            if loaded is not None:
                sets.append((ds, split, loaded[0], loaded[1]))
    if not sets:
        raise SystemExit("No usable prediction sets found; nothing to write.")

    n_expected = sum(len(q) for _, _, q, _ in sets)  # queries across all sets
    expected_ids = {qid for _, _, q, _ in sets for qid in q}

    # Merge mode: combine existing shard partials and exit (no ranking).
    if args.merge_shards:
        _merge_shards(out_path, args.merge_shards, n_expected, expected_ids)
        return

    # 2) Flatten to a deterministic global query list, tagging each with its pool's
    #    ranker. str_targets is built once per set and shared by reference.
    flat: List[Tuple[int, str, str, str, Dict[str, str], str]] = []
    set_str_targets = []
    for ds, split, queries, targets in sets:
        st = {tid: str(p) for tid, p in targets.items()}
        set_str_targets.append(st)
        for qid in sorted(queries):  # sort -> reproducible shard membership
            flat.append((ds, split, qid, str(queries[qid]), st, ranker_of_ds[ds]))

    # 3) Round-robin shard selection (so the ~140 expensive ds2 queries spread
    #    evenly across shards rather than piling into one contiguous chunk).
    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
    if not (1 <= i <= n):
        raise SystemExit(f"--query-shard {args.query_shard}: need 1 <= i <= N")
    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

    # 4) Upfront cost estimate over THIS shard's queries (reg pairs dominate; plain
    #    pairs are negligible). Print BEFORE any heavy work so it can be aborted.
    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")
    est_sec = reg_pairs * PER_REG_SEC
    print(f"\n[cost] shard {i}/{n}: {len(shard)} queries "
          f"({sum(rn=='reg_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} reg, "
          f"{sum(rn=='plain_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} plain)")
    print(f"[cost] {reg_pairs} registrations @ ~{PER_REG_SEC:.2f}s = "
          f"~{est_sec/60:.1f} min (~{est_sec/3600:.2f} h) projected wall-time "
          f"(+ plain MI negligible)")
    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
        rn = ranker_of_ds[ds]
        npairs = len(q) * len(t)
        tag = (f"~{npairs*PER_REG_SEC/60:.1f} min reg" if rn == "reg_mi"
               else "negligible (plain)")
        print(f"       dataset{ds}/{split} [{rn}]: {len(q)}x{len(t)} = {npairs} pairs -> {tag}")

    # 5) Build only the ranker instances this shard actually needs (RegMIRanker
    #    imports ANTs, so skip it if no reg pool is in play). Caches are per-instance
    #    and reused across the shard's queries.
    need = {rn for *_, rn in shard}
    rankers: Dict[str, object] = {}
    if "plain_mi" in need:
        rankers["plain_mi"] = MIRanker(grid=64)
    if "reg_mi" in need:
        rankers["reg_mi"] = RegMIRanker(grid=reg_grid, type_of_transform=args.transform)

    # 6) Sanity block (only shard 1, to avoid every container repeating it): prefer
    #    a reg pool (that's the risky ranker), else dataset1/val, else first set.
    if i == 1:
        sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d]) for d, s, q, t in sets
                       if ranker_of_ds[d] == "reg_mi"), None)
        if sanity is None:
            d, s, q, t = sets[0]
            sanity = (q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}", ranker_of_ds[d])
        _sanity_block(rankers[sanity[3]], sanity[0], sanity[1], sanity[2])

    # 7) Rank each query against ITS OWN pool's gallery only (pool isolation).
    print("\n=== ranking ===")
    t0 = time.time()
    rows: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
    for ds, split, qid, qpath, st_targets, rn in shard:
        ranking = rankers[rn].rank(qid, qpath, st_targets)  # full gallery best->worst
        # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
        rows.append({"query_id": qid, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
        if len(rows) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  ranked {len(rows)}/{len(shard)} queries "
                  f"({time.time() - t0:.1f}s elapsed)", flush=True)

    # 8) Write + verify. Single run (N=1) writes --out and must total n_expected;
    #    a shard writes a partial and must total this shard's query count.
    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=SUBMISSION_COLS).to_csv(dest, index=False)
    print(f"\nWrote {len(rows)} rows to {dest}  (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
    if n == 1:
        assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"total {len(rows)} != expected {n_expected}"
    else:
        print(f"Shard {i}/{n} done. Merge with: --merge-shards {n} --out {args.out}")

-    sanity = next(((q, t, f"dataset{d}/{s}") for d, s, q, t in sets
+    # 3) Round-robin shard selection (so the ~140 expensive ds2 queries spread

+    i, n = (int(x) for x in args.query_shard.split("/"))
+    shard = [row for idx, row in enumerate(flat) if idx % n == (i - 1)]

+    reg_pairs = sum(len(st) for _, _, _, _, st, rn in shard if rn == "reg_mi")

+          f"{sum(rn=='plain_mi' for *_, rn in shard)} plain)")
+    for ds, split, q, t in sets:
+    # 5) Build only the ranker instances this shard actually needs (RegMIRanker
+    #    imports ANTs, so skip it if no reg pool is in play). Caches are per-instance
+    #    and reused across the shard's queries.
+    # 6) Sanity block (only shard 1, to avoid every container repeating it): prefer
+    #    a reg pool (that's the risky ranker), else dataset1/val, else first set.

+        # Ranking length == gallery size: every target appears exactly once.
-    # 4) Write + verify. Row count must equal the queries we set out to rank.
-    assert len(rows) == n_expected, f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {n_expected}"
-        # Keep relative outputs anchored at the repo root, not the CWD.
+    # 8) Write + verify. Single run (N=1) writes --out and must total n_expected;
+    #    a shard writes a partial and must total this shard's query count.
+    assert len(rows) == len(shard), f"wrote {len(rows)} rows, expected {len(shard)}"
+    dest = out_path if n == 1 else _shard_path(out_path, i, n)

content.text: Cost estimate is exactly as expected: 11,600 regs (ds2 only), ~94.7 min; 377 total queries (140 reg / 237 plain). Now validate the full write/assert/coverage pipeline fast by running all-plain to a throwaway path (no ANTs), then check structure:

input.command:
cd /Users/alvaro/Documents/GitHub/ehl-paris-2026-medical-retrieval
source .venv/bin/activate
SCRATCH="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-alvaro-Documents-GitHub-ehl-paris-2026-medical-retrieval/d3e8fc6f-fd1d-48d3-8303-1980c07c6ebc/scratchpad"
echo "===== all-plain combined run (fast, exercises 377-row path) ====="
python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . \
  --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:plain_mi,3:plain_mi" \
  --out "$SCRATCH/allplain_full.csv" 2>&1 | grep -v -E "WARN .*missing image|^  ranked " | tail -15
echo "===== validate structure + pool isolation ====="
python - "$SCRATCH/allplain_full.csv" <<'PY'
import sys, pandas as pd
sub = pd.read_csv(sys.argv[1])
pools={}
for ds in (1,2,3):
    for sp in ("val","test"):
        q=pd.read_csv(f"dataset{ds}/{sp}_queries.csv")["query_id"].astype(str)
        g=pd.read_csv(f"dataset{ds}/{sp}_gallery.csv")["target_id"].astype(str)
        pools[(ds,sp)]=(set(q),set(g))
qid2pool={qid:(ds,sp) for (ds,sp),(qs,gs) in pools.items() for qid in qs}
bad_len=leak=dup=cov=0
for _,r in sub.iterrows():
    qid=str(r["query_id"]); rk=str(r["target_id_ranking"]).split()
    gs=pools[qid2pool[qid]][1]
    if len(rk)!=len(gs):bad_len+=1
    if set(rk)-gs:leak+=1
    if len(set(rk))!=len(rk):dup+=1
    if set(rk)!=gs:cov+=1
print("rows:",len(sub)," unique q:",sub.query_id.nunique())
print("bad_len",bad_len,"leak",leak,"dup",dup,"incomplete_cov",cov)
print("PASS" if (len(sub)==377 and not(bad_len or leak or dup or cov)) else "FAIL")
PY

input.command:

OUT="$SCRATCH/shardtest.csv"
for shard in 1/2 2/2; do
  python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . \
    --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:plain_mi,3:plain_mi" \
    --query-shard $shard --out "$OUT" 2>&1 | grep -E "shard $shard:|Wrote|Shard"
done
echo "--- merge ---"
python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . --merge-shards 2 --out "$OUT" 2>&1 | grep -E "Merged|mismatch|duplicate"
echo "--- verify merged == all-plain full ---"
python - "$OUT" "$SCRATCH/allplain_full.csv" <<'PY'
import sys,pandas as pd
a=pd.read_csv(sys.argv[1]).sort_values("query_id").reset_index(drop=True)
b=pd.read_csv(sys.argv[2]).sort_values("query_id").reset_index(drop=True)
print("merged rows:",len(a)," identical to single-run:", a.equals(b))
PY

content.text:
Everything is validated end-to-end without touching the long run:
- **Combined per-dataset map** → 377 rows, full pool isolation / full-length / no-dups / complete coverage.
- **Shard→merge** → shard 1/2 (189) + 2/2 (188) merge to 377 rows, **byte-identical** to the single run. Round-robin slicing and the merge coverage checks are correct.

## Upfront cost estimate (the real combined command)
```
[cost] shard 1/1: 377 queries (140 reg, 237 plain)
[cost] 11600 registrations @ ~0.49s = ~94.7 min (~1.58 h) projected wall-time (+ plain MI negligible)
       dataset1/val  [plain_mi]  40x40   -> negligible
       dataset1/test [plain_mi] 100x100  -> negligible
       dataset2/val  [reg_mi]    40x40   -> ~13.1 min reg
       dataset2/test [reg_mi]   100x100  -> ~81.7 min reg
       dataset3/val  [plain_mi]  20x20   -> negligible
       dataset3/test [plain_mi]  77x77   -> negligible
```

## My call: **local single run, ~95 min**
ANTs already multithreads each registration across ~6.7 local cores (confirmed: ds2-val logged 672% CPU), so local sharding gives **no speedup** — only resumability. Modal could fan out for a ~15-min wall time, but needs a fresh antspyx image, unverified data-on-volume, and remote auth/debug — risk I can't bound for a ~80-min theoretical gain on a one-off run. The shard/merge mechanism is built and tested if you later want Modal or a resumable local run.

**Exact command I'll launch (in background) on your go:**
```
python -m Alvaro.src.make_submission --data-root . \
  --ranker-map "1:plain_mi,2:reg_mi,3:plain_mi" --transform Affine \
  --out Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv
```

Stopping here for your confirmation, as requested.

content.text: Completed in 5852s (~97.5 min, matching the estimate). 377 rows written. Final structural validation:

input.command:
cd /Users/alvaro/Documents/GitHub/ehl-paris-2026-medical-retrieval
source .venv/bin/activate
python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
sub=pd.read_csv("Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv")
pools={}
for ds in (1,2,3):
    for sp in ("val","test"):
        q=pd.read_csv(f"dataset{ds}/{sp}_queries.csv")["query_id"].astype(str)
        g=pd.read_csv(f"dataset{ds}/{sp}_gallery.csv")["target_id"].astype(str)
        pools[(ds,sp)]=(set(q),set(g))
qid2pool={qid:(ds,sp) for (ds,sp),(qs,gs) in pools.items() for qid in qs}
expected={qid for qs,_ in pools.values() for qid in qs}
bad_len=leak=dup=cov=0
for _,r in sub.iterrows():
    qid=str(r["query_id"]); rk=str(r["target_id_ranking"]).split()
    gs=pools[qid2pool[qid]][1]
    if len(rk)!=len(gs):bad_len+=1
    if set(rk)-gs:leak+=1
    if len(set(rk))!=len(rk):dup+=1
    if set(rk)!=gs:cov+=1
print("rows:",len(sub)," unique q:",sub.query_id.nunique())
print("covers all 377 expected query ids:", set(sub.query_id.astype(str))==expected)
print("per-dataset row counts:", {ds:sum(qid2pool[str(x)][0]==ds for x in sub.query_id) for ds in (1,2,3)})
print("bad_len",bad_len,"cross_pool_leak",leak,"dup",dup,"incomplete_cov",cov)
print("ALL CHECKS PASS" if (len(sub)==377 and set(sub.query_id.astype(str))==expected and not(bad_len or leak or dup or cov)) else "FAIL")
PY

content.text:
The full combined submission is complete and validated.

## Result: `Alvaro/generated/regmi_full_submission.csv`
- **377 rows** — every required query (ds1 140, ds2 140, ds3 97), all unique, full coverage of the expected query set.
- **Runtime: 5852s (~97.5 min)** — on the ~95 min estimate (11,600 ds2 registrations; ds1/ds3 plain MI was negligible).
- **All structural checks pass:** every ranking is full-length for its pool, no duplicates, complete gallery coverage, **zero cross-pool leakage**. Valid Kaggle format.
- **Ranker mix applied as intended:** ds1/ds3 via plain MI (64³), ds2 via reg-MI affine (96³) — confirmed by the dataset2/val sanity block printing post-registration NMI with healthy top-tail spread (~0.05–0.08).

This is your one combined file to submit. Expected leaderboard behavior, based on the local proxy + the real ds2-val readout (0.884):
- **ds1**: plain MI was ~1.0 locally — should be strong on real ds1.
- **ds2**: affine reg-MI; real ds2-val already measured **0.884**, so ds2-test should land in that neighborhood.
- **ds3**: plain MI ~0.98 locally — should be strong, with the caveat that real ds3 (genuine pre-op→intra-op) may be harder than the proxy.

Submit `regmi_full_submission.csv` and tell me the per-dataset numbers. If real ds3 underperforms the proxy, that's the next lever (ds3 is currently plain MI — a content-robust approach or light registration there would be the follow-up). The shard/merge and Modal-ready path is in place if you later want faster iteration or to add SyN on a specific pool.
